title: Patterson Lake Corridor claim-gap uranium-gold-critical-mineral screening

aoi.name: Patterson Lake Corridor, southwest Athabasca Basin margin, Saskatchewan, Canada
aoi.crs: EPSG:4326 for downloaded outputs; source Saskatchewan layers wkid 2151/latest 2957

ranked_findings.commodity_model: basement-hosted high-grade uranium with REE-Th-Ni-Cu-Co accessory evidence
ranked_findings.claim_gap_status: mostly active tenure in this screen
ranked_findings.recommended_entry: JV, option, acquisition, boundary and good-standing watchlist
JV/option or future boundary gap monitoring

ranked_findings.claim_gap_status: very low probability of unclaimed core ground
ranked_findings.recommended_entry: corporate transaction or non-core claim acquisition

ranked_findings.commodity_model: REE-Th plus Ni-Cu-Co and weak uranium optionality
ranked_findings.claim_gap_status: lapsed features present but active bbox intersections also present; exact MARS subtraction required
ranked_findings.recommended_entry: immediate tenure subtraction and MARS verification

ranked_findings.claim_gap_status: no active disposition bbox intersection in local grid screen

key_caveats: This is not a Qualified Person opinion, title opinion, mineral resource estimate, reserve estimate, mine plan, or investment recommendation.
Lapsed layer did not expose lapse date, so recent expiry within one to two years cannot be confirmed from the downloaded public layer alone.
The grid screen used bbox overlaps, not exact polygon overlay, for active and lapsed tenure.
MARS parcel-level verification is mandatory before staking or acquisition decisions.
Gold evidence in the AOI is mostly accessory or isolated drillhole evidence, not enough for a standalone gold-belt thesis.
